reviews.review_description:
This is a great game, and a brilliant use of VR and hand tracking technology. 

 The choice of music is generally excellent.  On most of the tracks, the required hand movements do a good job of creating an illusion of leading the orchestra (the player doesn't actually change the music unless they mess up).   Changes in intensity, tempo and complexity make you feel like you're actively engaging with music while also fighting your way through a video game level. Prokofiev's "Battle on the Ice"  feels like a battle, Hans Zimmer's "Jack Sparrow" feels like an adventure, and "In the Hall of the Mountain King" builds from a quiet and stealthy beginning to a hectic finish. It's a pretty unique game experience.

Hard mode seemed overwhelming at first, but with a little practice I was able to get five stars on most of the selections in that mode. It's definitely more satisfying than the lower difficulties once you get the hang of it.

There are a few issues and things I might change, but I don't want to nitpick. Stepping into an imaginary concert hall and conducting the overture from "The Marriage of Figaro" before heading to work in the morning  feels like living in the future, in a good way. The creators should be congratulated for doing something new, and for doing it so well.

reviews.review_title: I love this game so so much, but…
reviews.review_description: Every time I play, I use my baton. But! I think you should make it more realistic, like for the 4/4 time, you go in an arrow. Down, up halfway, down, diagonally up left, down diagonally right, then diagonally up right, diagonally down right, then back up. I think you should also make it to where you can also see the actual music on the paper and could actually move the pages if it’s a long one. I think you should also make like a concert mode to where it’s just non stop playing and you choose the songs you play, kinda like a loadout. I think you should also like let us search up other songs like pomp and circumstance. Or other British ones, because I’d love to conduct those.

The most original VR rhythm game.
Intuitive controls, amazing customizations and a genre of music never before approached in a rhythm game.
Absolutely brilliant.
I don’t need things all be the same, I enjoy having a real variety of imaginative creations, that use different approaches and different genres of music. Like the most respected classical pieces of all time. Simple minds dislike differences, and would prefer to keep things the same as they already know. Intelligent people enjoy exploring and broadening their horizons. Which kind of person are you? 😀🤘🏻

The music is what makes Maestro what it is. Please never add any music that does not fit the Maestro aesthetic

reviews.review_title: Fantastic App Held Back by Music
reviews.review_description:
This is one of those special apps that is artistic, fun, and innovative.  It’s a rhythm game, but instead of swatting at boxes you’ll be directing a fully visualized and impressive orchestra, via flicking your baton to keep tempo, with the dominant hand, while the off-hand points to activate parts of the orchestra And raising and lowering your palm to modulate their volume.  It doesn’t sound like much but hard is SUPER intense.

The best part of this game is the immersive details.  You are fully surrounded by an auditorium, with orchestra before you and audience behind.  The graphics and audio quality are great.  At the end of a song, the audience throws roses (or tomatoes), depending on the quality of your performance, along with clapping or jeering.  Social media comments also pop up in the air.  You can pick up roses off the floor and throw them back to the crowd (acquired skill) or lose your chill and smash your wine glass in celebration or disgust.  The whole production is very immersive.

Each performance unlocks more songs, stages, outfits for your players, and skins for your gloves and baton, some of which are quite fun.  I’m particularly impressed with the Heaven vs hell and battlefield stages/outfits, complete with spurting lava and cannons firing during the song!

There is also a good tutorial at the start; you won’t be confused about what to do.  And the best part is that no controllers are needed!  The hand-tracking works fabulously and adds a lot of immersion and fun.  Just remember to hold your baton and make your movements as pompously expressive as possible (that pinky better be out!), for maximum effect!

So, why only 4 stars?  Unfortunately, the musical selection really limits the experience.  While there are a couple jazz songs, all the rest are classical, which have a lot of very slow, boring parts in them and all feel quite similar.  The lack of other genres is only made more pronounced by the fact that the tutorial includes multiple modern songs, including rap, and they were amazing!  But you only get to experience those for like 30 seconds and then there’s nothing but far less engaging classical…  WtF devs?  Don’t tease us; give me more of the tutorial!  I would absolutely pay for a pack of music like that; it was sick!  I would also love to see the cannon-shots sync up with heavy bass notes in the songs, so they’re actually part of it, rather than background noise.

And that’s pretty much it; it just needs better, more varied music!  Otherwise, it’s fantastic.  It plays very well, the atmosphere is excellent.  I wasn’t expecting the stage and costumes to change or be somewhat interactive; that was a very pleasant surprise.  I sincerely hope they add some DLC non-classical music packs (as well as a few free ones), bundled with themed stages and costumes that react to the music.  My wallet is ready to support!

I’ve played this game for around 2 years and I’ve never seen anything like it. It’s beautifully designed and unique. The music quality and just quality in general is outstanding. Completely recommend.

Really amazing game! I really enjoy playing it. There's just two things though.

1. A lot of songs are locked behind purchasable packs. This is the reason I have the rating at 4 stars. If there was a way to unlock songs from these packs over time (currency, etc.) I would bump the rating up to 5 stars.
2. Something that I think could be really nice is the ability to compose your own music. Sure, this might take a while to implement, but it would be a pretty nice feature! (This could be done through a website, mobile app, steam, or in the game itself.)
Overall, this game is pretty good! Something I would also like to see is a concert band (not orchestra), that way you could have more options for music.

Love this whole game it’s does well at being a rhythm ￼game and all the stuff u could do like getting new stages to having new clothes for ur band mates all in all a sold game and just disappointed they didn’t add CHRISTMAS music.

I really love this game!
As a band kid, I love conducting my own band and listening to the music. In a way, I’m practicing my instrument!
But I do have a suggestion.
Even though it doesn’t really fit the game, I would love a marching band version. I LOVE marching season and feeling the chills of cranking with my friends and having fun. I’ve always wanted to listen to a band play while I guide them. It brings me chills just thinking about it.
I doubt it will happen because it is very different from the vibe of a renaissance era orchestra or smth but still lol😭😭✌️✌️✌️

developer_response.body: Marching band is - of course - coming.

reviews.review_description: Let me start off saying how much I enjoy this game! I have gotten 5 stars on all the songs on Easy and Normal(Yay!) So when I started on the Hard mode it was well...hard! This is when I started looking online for tips and tricks to help with game play. I discovered the sites kept referring to different  Batons that are rewarded with 90% or 100% accuracy on certain songs that would offer different abilities. Is this true? I have a Meta Quest2. Is this feature not available for Quest2  users? I only have the 1st Baton, feather, spoon and cigarette available not the 10 other ones mentioned online that are unlockable.

Maestro is a fun and fairly unique music rhythm game. It's especially nice having the chance to work with classical music. Admittedly the general gameplay is technically quite limited to a few movements but it makes you feel somewhat engaged with the music.

The environments/sets are pretty cool although you tend to be laser focused on the cues and can't afford to take them in properly while conducting.

My main criticism of the game comes from the failure to include all actions in the different difficulty settings. While the demo/tutorial clues you into the different motions, including both crescendo and diminuendo (raise and lower palm gestures), normal difficulty seems to only include crescendo. Moving up to hard both increases pace and includes the dim gesture. For me that translates to trying to include something new and unfamiliar at a pace that is too awkward to match. Whereas if it were included in the lower difficulty the 'learning' or 'memory' part is already done and it's just the increased volume of gestures to get to grips with. Not only that, it's just odd having a gesture missing altogether and kind of takes away from the limited gameplay.

I admit I struggle with some suggested motions; I'm not sure if it's just me. For example there's often double arrows down, followed by down and 'out' followed by double arrows down and 'in'. Well after I've done the first one my hands are down, I can't lower them any further while changing direction, I have to raise them to do the next action, and that often leads to errors.

I also find that every now and then the pointing circles are out of my periphery and can be missed due to no fault of my own. And sometimes the timing with those feels off too.

Despite these niggles, I really enjoy playing the game and it feels quite satisfying to successfully get through a beautiful piece of music and be cheered by the crowd.

Music wise, I'm happy with the concentration on mostly classical. I'd love to see more baroque pieces added. However some of the more contemporary music seen mostly during the demo/tutorial works surprisingly well and it would be interesting to see some expansion in that area, within reason. Also, the big band/swing stuff is awesome, more of that would be really welcome.

As I always wanted to have the experience of conducting an orchestra, this game scratches that itch perfectly. 

I have nog idea if this game comes close to the real thing. I would imagine that in real life the orchestra would follow the conductor, the game seems more focused on the conductor following the 'happy flow' of the orchestra. Maybe there is some room for improvement. Would love to be able to influence the speed the orchestra plays a composition, give the piece my own 'flavor'. As it is now a composition is more or less 'on rails'.

Periodically new DLC's drop in, which I immediately buy to try-out the new compositions. First in easy mode to explore the musical landscape, after that in normal mode to further develop my skills. Hard mode usually ends in rotten tomatoes. 😅 The scope of the compositions is expanding through the DLC's. Would love to see soms more contemporary musical pieces, though. Mayby some rap or rock inspired pieces. Tupac? Or Bohemian Rhapsody! 😄 Whatever you decide, just come take my money.

Good job devs. 💪🏻
The hand tracking is bad and it makes u just wanna break the headset don’t install this game if u hate wasting ur time

developer_response.body: Hey we're very sorry to read this, this is very unusual feedback! Can you tell us more about the issue you encountered? What Headset are you playing on? We can certainly help!

reviews.review_description: Could you guys add the halo theme song in the game?

developer_response.body: Trying to, but "it's complicated".

reviews.review_description: Impossible to have a good grade when the game has a bad hand tracking

reviews.review_title: Dream Game has come to life
reviews.review_description:
I have always dreamed of something like this ever since I started playing on VR with the Quest

I love the gameplay the tracking is very smooth and intuitive and the selection of music albeit small is amazing

I never thought in a hundred years I would be ever conducting Duel of the Fates in VR and let me tell you it was the most beautiful experience I have EVER enjoyed on VR

If you are in thinking about getting the game here or on steam get the bundle with all DLC you won’t be disappointed in the least this game is worth its weight in gold 

Hope to see more music in the future but in the meantime this is a masterpiece

reviews.review_title: I hope to be able to play with one hand
reviews.review_description: I truly enjoyed the musical feast this game provided. I want my sick mother to experience it too, as she can only use it with one hand and one eye due to brain damage. I hope they can develop an independent system for her.

I will 5 star ONLY if you add these things:
1: imperial march
2: Jurrasic Park stuff
3: “Anikans Dark Deeds” Revenge of the Sith
That’s like it. :)

ADD INTERSTELLAR. Side note: I really think the game would benefit from adding more movie soundtrack pieces. You did Duel of the Fates, what about Test Drive from how to train your dragon? Or adding The Planets by Holst? Pirates of the Caribbean? Spider-Man? I think I already made Interstellar a point lol. There are so many good pieces out there and as a musican who craves the dream of preforming, this is all I really have right now and I'm sure other people are in the same boat as me. Of not being able to chase their dreams due to financial reasons. But this game has potential.

developer_response.body:
Well
Holt's Mars from "The Planets"' is in our free DLC "La Crème de la Crème", and Pirates of the Carribbean has two pieces in our latest DLC "All Aboard"!

reviews.review_description:
I absolutely love this game. I grew up as a band geek, and loved playing the classics. I wish there were more jazz pieces. But I know you can’t have everything. I can’t wait for the sequel. I think so much more could be done with the orchestra. Right now they feel like animatronics on a Disney ride. But, as gameplay goes;￼ I love it. 

In order to immerse myself into the virtual reality, I hold a chopstick in the place of my baton. It makes it feel much more real, and I feel like my physical hand is matching the digital hand. If that makes sense. ￼
The game is great it is innovative and unique even though most popular songs are locked behind an addon

￼I’ve had this game for quite a while and I think it’s really good. I do prefer playing VR sitting down though and it’s a pain to have to turn your whole body around to view the stage when you get your praise from the audience or to initiate some key things in the game. 

This I would think is a very easy fix, even if it’s an option in the menu just in case people prefer the original experience. 

Not everybody likes to stand or has the ability to in order to play this game. Allowing either one or both of the joysticks to turn your character around using either snap or smooth turning would be fantastic.

That being said, the game is great if you like rhythm based games and If you ever wanted to take a crack at being a maestro yourself, this game provides a really cool experience. The controls are responsive and the ability to hold a stick in your hand, really adds to the immersion.

Devs, please consider adding either the ability to turn using the sticks or to have it as an option in the options menu. It will give more people access to your game. This game would be boosted to five stars if this was the case.

reviews.review_title: I enjoyed the first time but
reviews.review_description: I went into it again and found I needed help with getting set up again. I was not able to find a help option or an option for learning how to use.

developer_response.body:
Hey you can scroll at the top of the tracklist to re-play "The Masterclass", it's the tutorial that will teach you everything!

EDIT: Checking up on you, did you manage to find how to re-play the tutorial?

reviews.review_description: so heres the thing i love this game but when in the tutorial I'm on the cues part  and Then when I point it doesn't work. So i have no reason too play it... Which is sad cuz loved the other parts of the tutorial second is that even if i don't use hand tracking i cant even POINT. With my free hand when i use controllers. Anyhow this game is amazing and fun too play! please fix these bugs too help my gameplay better :)

developer_response.body: Hey the cues need to be pointed at just when the circle closes! If you hold your hand too soon in advance, this may not register as a correct gesture! Can you try timing your "point" motion closer to when the circle closes and let us know? We hope this helps!

reviews.review_description:
The idea is good but the game seems to be as intelligent as hitting animals when they appears from a hole. The point of the game is not conducting orchesta but following marks on the screen. Orchestra is just a nice background. 

Suggestions: orchestra should follow conductor and not otherwise. Notes that are useful. Instead of arrows on the screen can have highlights in notes for example dynamics and tempo changes. Possibility to send the stupid assistant away.

After playing the game and buying all the dlcs, I’ve had lots of fun conducting cool pieces of music! I wish In the future that they release a dlc that has more space-themed songs or Christmas-themed songs because that would be awesome!

reviews.review_title: The greatest VR game I’ve played.
reviews.review_description: As a musician, it’s clear that somebody, somewhere, really cares about this game and its concept. It’s expertly made, down to the minute half beats and time changes. I’ve never been so impressed. To feel so totally in control of an orchestra, and yet feel the stress of a conductor on hard mode is a feeling I can’t quite describe. Will be buying all current and future DLCs. I literally have zero bad feedback outside of pressing buttons being hit or miss but with hand tracking it happens. Love it.
No i didnt say vr game i mean my favorite game in general. It feels so good to direct a whole orchestra. And the songs are so catchy. Buy this game you wont regret it!

I enjoyed this game so much. More than I expected to. My only complaint is that there isn't an option to play while sitting. I don't always like to stand in VR for long periods of time so I would play this game for longer if there was that option. That would make it 5 stars.

developer_response.body:
Hey thanks for the review!
You can find the seated option in the Settings of the game :)

reviews.review_title: Buy it; reminds me of rock band
reviews.review_description: Just absolutely buy it, enjoying it a lot, this is a genius idea, especially when it comes to VR and the hand tracking, i got it with the horizon+, I’ll definitely be buying the game and the DLCs. Reminds me of rockband and guitar hero but instead you’re a composer, it’s so fun, I also downloaded the game because of the lord of the rings and pirates of the Caribbean. So please devs, add in more LOTR music tracks and themes maybe? Thank you for making this game, I’ll buy every single dlc for this game and every game like this. Customers, support these devs, the passion behind this game is unreal, loved the tutorial as well, magnificent game!

reviews.review_title: Great gameplay and selection of music
reviews.review_description: This is a great game and seems to work fine playing seated for me, despite what some other reviews say. The game includes a pretty good selection of classical music, which I love.  This is a really outstanding gameplay idea to make use of the Quest 3 hand-tracking.  I would definitely recommend this game to people who used to play in an orchestra in high school or college.
The only thing it’s missing is phantom of the opera

The developers of this game have out done themselves. I am a professional musician who when to school for music performance and let me tell you....they nailed the feel of conducting. Is every pattern perfect? There are only a couple instances where I may not normally conduct a passage that way, but how they have it, it is still VERY smooth.  If you want to live your dream of being a conductor, check this game out. Its VERY fun. (Especially on hard mode! The patterns feel VERY fluid!) Looking forward to more songs! Bravo!

I discovered this gem today and was blown away by how fun, engaging and intuitive it is. 

I never knew i wanted to be a conductor, but here I am, and I keep coming back for more. 

Highly recommend giving it a try.
This game is amazing it’s so incredibly fun but plz plz add Interstellar it’d be the best thing to have on here for this game
Quite funny at the beginning. I don't get the hard mode. How can you hold a note with left arm while using both to hit a series of doble arrows at the centre?

developer_response.body: Hit them with your left palm staying open!

reviews.review_description: My only gripe with the game was that there wasn't a left-handed option. I understand that they would have to flip everything to accommodate 10% of the users, but they said they will make this change soon. I changed my rating, but when I clicked on the 3-star reviews, my original post was still there. This game is absolutely amazing! I think once they put the lefty option in, I could play this on hard. WHEN WILL MY PEOPLE BE ACKNOWLEDGE? LEFTY OPTION NOW!
Its absolute peak

reviews.review_title: Possibly the best app I’ve ever played
reviews.review_description: If you love music and you love beat saber, this is a must have app. The visuals are stunning. It truly feels like you are conducting great scores, and the hand tracking is superb. Now developers need to make a broadway song list available. Les Mis, Phantom, Miss Saigon, Wicked, and the classics. You will make a fortune if you get Cameron Mackintosh and Andrew Lloyd Webber on board😄!!

developer_response.body: Thanks a lot for the review! Those pieces are indeed in our sights, you should definitely join our Discord to suggest more: we take requests :)

reviews.review_description:
This is a fun game that allows anyone from amateur to experienced composed. It provides many iconic songs to play. It has costumization for gloves, costumes, baton, and your stage ( sad to say its only the stage). I do have some recommendations and complaints though.
1. Not enough songs
2. Too little options to costumize
3. I agree with the other users, there should be a sitting option
4. The guy who serves you after the song is lokey creepy
5. The environment should include the audience
6.There should be a duel mode
7. Can you add more songs from Fantasia?
8. We need more songs with choral accompaniment 

FireInTheBowl, there is a lefty option in the settings for the game.
 
If you are debating getting this game, I got it on sale, and it was worth the money I bought it for. Full price, its too much money for too little songs. I do love this game though.

developer_response.body:
Hey thanks a lot for the review! There is a seated option currently actionnable in the settings, but the turn around controls are being worked on (we need to make them work with both controllers and hand tracking, which isn't super obvious to do well, but it's solved an will be released in the next update!)

Have you gotten the free DLC with 5 additional tracks and two cosmetics?

reviews.review_description: Love the music. Would love to see more songs added. Like interstellar or other great songs.

I love the songs in here, it's awesome. Please add expert mode or insane mode please 👍

edit: you have said that the insane mode is out as a reply... I don't see it

developer_response.body: Insane mode is our default mode.

reviews.review_description: One of the best games on the whole Meta store.

This game is phenomenal, especially with the constant updates and a great variety of music genres. The orchestra and the costumes look fantastic—the immersion is real.
Erik is the star of the show! That colorful French guy is so charismatic. The way he brings you a glass of champagne after a successful performance is such a fun and unique touch. It really brings a smile to my face every time.
One suggestion for the developers: it would be amazing to have a live online audience. I’m not sure if it's technically possible, but having real people watching your concert would take it to a whole new level! Anyway, the game is a must-buy!

As a professional musician and actual orchestral conductor, I was expecting this game to be a humorous adaptation of games like Beat Saber. I was amazed to find how closely it resembles actual conducting and what great fun it is! The departures from actual conducting technique, such as conducted accents coinciding with the accented notes and conducting on syncopated beats are completely understandable and in no way seem out of place. Proper conducting patterns, cues given well in advance of entrances, crescendi, diminuendi and fermati are all brilliantly integrated. I can't recommend this app highly enough!
This game is absolute peak and I love it. It just needs some more songs. Overall, this game is great!

Maestro VR is a fantastic concept — conducting a live orchestra in VR feels amazing and immersive. The baton mechanics and sense of presence are spot-on, but the experience still feels too limited. The song selection is short, and I’d love to see full orchestral shows with more variety and emotion. Not every performance should run smoothly either — give the conductor and stage crew moments to adapt or improvise through unexpected challenges.

There’s huge potential for deeper storytelling. Imagine performing for political figures or touring internationally, where each musical choice shapes your reputation or even sparks controversy. That kind of narrative risk would make every concert feel meaningful and alive.

I’d also love to connect more with the orchestra — maybe through rehearsals, notes, that reveal their lives and personalities. Some members might struggle with personal or financial issues, as the conductor, your decisions could help them: choosing a favorite song to lift their mood, giving them a solo to restore confidence, or boosting orchestra’s fame so they can afford new instruments or more time at home. That human element would make each performance feel earned, and give the progression.

The snobby French character is great but underused. His dialogue feels repetitive and often disconnected from what’s happening. If his comments reacted to your actions — discussing music when you browse pieces, costumes when you’re backstage, theatre gossip during show planning — he’d feel more alive. After all, he’s your quiet defender within the theatre: knowledgeable, connected, and precise. If you earn his respect, he’ll make sure you succeed — his wit and good taste could become essential (even if you battle out a few selections, you come to respect eachother!)

Maestro VR already captures the magic of conducting beautifully. It just needs more story, character interaction, and emotional depth to become something truly unforgettable.

developer_response.body:
Thanks a lot for the review! You'll be pleased to know that a very significant portion of of your suggestions are on the drawing board :3
Join our Discord: https://discord.gg/X8cj3sC4Gt

reviews.review_description: I played the demo because it was on TikTok and I loved it and now that its out and I got the game and played it I loved everything I was fully aggressive with the accents and everything I'm so glad someone made a game like this even tho I didn't wish for a game like this, definitely get it, it's worth it and if you can't afford it it's on the horizon+ at least now or wait for a sale

This is an absolutely terrific game, and I’ve scored magnificent on all the numbers, playing about an hour a day since I got it. Only a few suggestions, and one is to not go overboard on the soundtracks. So many soundtracks
, including these, are repetitive and not all that much fun to conduct. Please stick to the classical. It would also be nice to have longer pieces, and the ability to combine several numbers into a full concert. But I love what you’re doing and keep coming out with more music! ￼

This is an amazing app. It also manages to give you some exercise while you’re having a time of your life practising being in front of an orchestra. So beautiful to look at but also very very immersive and engaging! Bravo to the developers! This is really a wonderful app that I look forward to have many more hours of fun with!

From the moment I loaded up I knew that I was going to enjoy this immersive experience. If you like the theme and the music 🎶 then this is a must purchase. However, One feature that is missing and really should be there is the ability to create a concert of say at least 3 songs together. Either randomly chosen or player chosen. Finally… More music tracks please. Keep them coming 😁👍🏻🎶

As a classical music lover (especially movie scores) this is so much fun. My new favorite game to play on quest 2. One recommendation - Get Hans Zimmer and John Williams on the phone asap!! We need their catalogs of music on here!!
This game is very fun, but the thing is, when I put my palm down with hand tracking, it said I did it wrong?? But hey, anything other than that, the game is very fun
Can play seated but need a seated rotation option. Still super dope

I've had this game for a while now, and I never get bored of playing it. I have a blast every time, usually ending with a silly happy face that probably means something like "hahahahahah, I just 'conducted' The Lord of the Rings score, hahahaahaa." 😁😁😁 It's soo muucchh fuunn and I hope there's more LOTR and John Williams' music coming (and maybe some Alan Silvestri?). Point is, this game isssss greeeaaattttt!!!!😁😁😁
Love the song choices just wish there were more free ones

We need phantom of the Opera songs and Tim Burton songs, please. I've been playing this like crazy and I love orchestra music. But if I was able to play phantom of the Opera and multiple different Tim Burton songs from multiple different movies, I would be in heaven.

P. S. You're a magnificent human being and this game is something alive always wanted but never knew it until now. I'll have every bundle soon! 🥰

developer_response.body: Will do.

reviews.review_description: Love this experience and the pieces! I would LOVE phantom of the opera to be included as a DLC pack in the future.
Brilliant game. Love the details! Allows left handed after tutorial. Would be great to choose from the start. Really fun though.

id love some features where people can make custom wands and songs and stages, if there is such stuff song wise for quest 3 ifs nit showing up, like imagine alic in wonderland stage and stuff like that, overall amazing and fun game so cool i love it, dare i say but better then beat saber, it would tho be better if it had more moddec features. Please consider my request i beg please.

developer_response.body:
Hey thanks for the reviews! Custom maps are coming :3 Join our Discord to checkout the roadmap for Maestro!!!

https://discord.gg/zrh4qb4Qap

reviews.review_description: Overall, it is absolutely amazing. Sometimes I feel there aren't enough songs, but I also know that adding each song wasn't easy. Because the gameplay experience for each song is exquisite.  So it's fine.
Over all a fun interactive game enjoy the music and game play and as someone who goes to the Detroit orchestra a lot it’s a fun change to see the orchestra from the conductor point of view !

reviews.review_title: Probably one of my favorite games in VR
reviews.review_description: I’m sure this game isn’t fully accurate to the real thing, but it’s immersive and engaging. You can almost get a work out depending on how much you get into it. I never leave comments but I had to with this game. I also never leave requests and I have to ask. Since Justin Hurwitz Whiplash music is on here, can we please get Lala Land  music as well. I don’t mean to get greedy 😂

developer_response.body:
This game is actually FULLY accurate.
And yes more jazz will come.

reviews.review_description:
I really enjoyed this game, but I would like to see more Star Wars and other John Williams music if that’s at all possible.￼

That said, this is a really fun game that makes it feel like you’re conducting a symphony

Played the full game but sadly all of the more popular music is locked behind pay walls. Just wish you could pay a set fee and pick the music you'd like to play. But micro transactions make more money I guess.

I thought this looked fun but was not sure if I would really like it, and then it I noticed it on Horizon+ so I gave it a try. Thanks for that Meta because I may have missed out on a lot of fun by not trying this. Found out right off that Maestro had hand tracking, and I mean really really good hand tracking on fast movements and it lets you move how you want as long as you are hitting the prompts in the right directions. I wear a big goofy grin through every song and enjoy the music selection.

I’m giving this 5 stars even with its flaws. First I will say that it’s 5 stars as a party game but 4 stars as a single player game. Let me explain:
  I had no problems with hand tracking whatsoever as some people had. The music could use a larger library. But the gameplay was great; especially when sharing the headset with other people. If they suck, you’ll hear boos and instruments scratching. It’s a fun game.
  Now as a single person game, there’s really no campaign or story. Yeah it’s fun but there’s nothing there to push you on. I would have liked a story line to where you’re trying to play at Carnegie hall or something like that, but it’s not there. That’s my only gripe
But the execution itself and the level of difficulty.... Ehm, for children

Fantastic game! My only constructive criticism is that I wish it were more like a professional simulator and less “pretend”. Also a bit too many loading screens, I don’t like having to go back to the main menu between songs.
Just buy it

reviews.review_description: Best game i have ever played

I really like Maestro overall — it’s a fun and unique VR experience that makes you actually feel like you’re conducting music. The visuals and idea are great, and when it works, it’s super satisfying.

That said, it’s so frustrating how inconsistent the calibration is. Half the time it doesn’t pick up when I’m clearly pointing at something, even when I’m right on target. Then other times, it works perfectly — only to suddenly stop again when I do the exact same motion. It kind of ruins the rhythm and flow of the game when you’re constantly fighting the tracking instead of enjoying the music.

I still think it’s worth trying, but I really hope the developers fix the detection or add better calibration options in a future update because at points it does make me regret buying the game.

I am playing on the quest 3s and I use the controllers

developer_response.body:
Ok on Quest 3s with controllers you shouldn't be experiencing any issues that we know of.

You seem to be describing issues with the "cue" mechanic (i.e the shrinking white circles you are required to point at). I have two questions to make sure you always perform them as the game expects:
- 1 Are you making sure to use your free hand, and not your baton hand to point at them?
- 2 Are you pointing them just when they are completely shrunk (which you should), or are you pointing way in advance, and letting your static towards them (big no no)?

Please let us know, we're happy to help!

EDIT : Hello Alice just checking on you!
